---
title: "Math"
author: "Chao Ma"
date: "2026-04-08"
---

Notes across probability, calculus, linear algebra, and optimization, organized as one mathematical foundation rather than separate silos.

---

::: {.content-grid}

::: {.content-card}
**[Probability](Probability/index.qmd)**
MIT 6.041 notes on sample spaces, axioms, discrete and continuous models, and the basic laws that make probability a mathematical system.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[Calculus](Calculus/index.qmd)**
Gilbert Strang's calculus notes on derivatives, integrals, differential equations, Taylor series, and the structural ideas behind first-year calculus.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[MIT 18.06SC Linear Algebra](MIT18.06/lectures.qmd)**
Core linear algebra through subspaces, orthogonality, eigenvalues, determinants, Fourier, and geometric intuition, plus synthesis notes.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[MIT 18.065: Linear Algebra Applications](MIT18.065/lectures.qmd)**
Linear algebra in data analysis, optimization, neural nets, probability, convolution, Fourier, and matrix methods for machine learning.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[Stanford EE 364A: Convex Optimization](EE364A/lectures.qmd)**
Convex sets, convex functions, duality, conic viewpoints, and optimization theory with a systems-and-geometry perspective.
:::

:::
